topic: The dark truth about conformity — Asch's line experiment

script.title: Why You'd Agree With a Lie

scenes.narration: Three out of four people will deny what their own eyes see if the room disagrees. You are not the exception. You are the pattern.
scenes.image_prompt: Three empty wooden chairs in a row facing a single chair, stark interrogation lighting, black and white film noir, long shadows stretching across a bare floor

scenes.narration: In 1951, Solomon Asch drew three lines on a card. One was obviously longer. He asked a simple question. Then he broke us.
scenes.image_prompt: A yellowed index card on a wooden desk with three vertical black lines of different lengths, harsh desk lamp, black and white film noir, grainy texture

scenes.narration: Actors in the room gave the wrong answer on purpose. The real subject sat last. Their eyes told the truth. Their mouth betrayed them.
scenes.image_prompt: A single mouth half hidden in shadow, teeth slightly parted, a hand covering the lower face, black and white film noir, heavy vignette, extreme close up

scenes.narration: Seventy-five percent caved at least once. Intelligent people. Confident people. They watched reality, then agreed with the lie to belong.
scenes.image_prompt: A cracked mirror on a concrete wall reflecting a room full of identical empty chairs, jagged glass fractures, black and white film noir, low angle

scenes.narration: Your brain treats social rejection like physical injury. Disagreeing with a group lights up the same region as a broken bone.
scenes.image_prompt: A single hand gripping the edge of a table, knuckles bone white, chains coiled loosely around the wrist, black and white film noir, harsh side lighting

scenes.narration: This is how bad ideas spread. How entire companies miss obvious frauds. How juries convict the innocent. Nobody speaks. Everyone sees.
scenes.image_prompt: A long empty courtroom bench, wooden gavel resting on its side, heavy shadows falling across polished wood, black and white film noir, deep perspective

scenes.narration: Add one dissenting voice and the spell breaks. When a single person disagrees, conformity collapses by eighty percent. Courage is contagious.
scenes.image_prompt: A single locked door slightly ajar with dim light bleeding through the crack, heavy iron chains dangling from the handle, black and white film noir

scenes.narration: The next time the room nods, notice what your gut said first. Silence is not agreement. Silence is how the lie wins. You already know.
scenes.image_prompt: A closed mouth with a single fingertip pressed vertically against the lips, deep shadows across the face, black and white film noir, tight crop
